description: Is the conceptual-bridge detector (edit_distance_bridges, the 200 MIN-closest cross-cluster proof pairs) matching genuine shared domain-specific structure or just GENERIC scaffolding? Tests a-0033's hypothesis directly and cheaply over existing data. Token genericity = number of distinct Leiden clusters a normalized tactic token appears in (over all >=5-tactic proofs). For each conceptual-bridge pair compute the multiset of SHARED tokens and classify: generic (>=30 of ~138 clusters) vs domain-specific (<=3 clusters). Control = random cross-cluster proof pairs from the SAME top-30 x top-30 longest-proof selection the detector used (20 deterministic draws per cluster pair). Pure stdlib, reuses data/theorem_tactics.json + clusters.json + cluster_labels.json + normalize_tactics.
motivation: a-0033 (e-0037) found the operational/conceptual bridge independence is specific to MIN aggregation and HYPOTHESIZED the MIN-closest pairs are 'generic-scaffolding coincidences' not Brouwer<->Lawvere-style shared concepts -- asserted but never measured. If the conceptual detector's matched structure is ubiquitous scaffolding (intro/simp/exact/rw), it cannot recover genuine conceptual bridges, concretely sharpening the central open problem (q-0009/a-0001). Falsifiable: if MIN-closest pairs share MORE domain-specific tokens than random cross-cluster pairs, the detector carries real conceptual content; if not, a-0033 is confirmed.
formal_results: 15,777 proofs (>=5 tactics), 81,319 distinct normalized tokens, ~138 clusters. Most generic tokens: 'rw [@]' (38 clusters), 'apply @' (36), 'constructor' (34), 'intro ?' (33), 'exact @'/'simp only [@,@]'/'rw [@,@]'/'exact ?' (29). Conceptual bridges (200 MIN-closest pairs) vs random cross-cluster control: mean shared-token mass 4.105 vs 0.724; mean generic fraction of shared mass 0.446 vs 0.540; median generic frac 0.50 vs 0.50; frac of pairs sharing >=1 domain-specific (<=3 cluster) token 0.005 (1/200) vs 0.0005; mean #specific shared tokens 0.005 vs 0.0007. Threshold sensitivity for the bridges: specific<=1 cluster 0/200, <=3 1/200, <=5 2/200, <=8 3/200, <=10 3/200. The single specific-sharing pair matches on 'intro epsilon epspos' (an analysis epsilon-intro idiom).
results_description: The MIN-closest conceptual-bridge pairs DO share more tactic tokens than random cross-cluster pairs (4.1 vs 0.7), so the detector is not matching noise -- but that shared structure is essentially all generic-to-midfrequency scaffolding. Only 1/200 conceptual-bridge pairs shares even a single domain-specific (<=3 of 138 clusters) tactic token, statistically indistinguishable from the random rate, and robust to the threshold (relaxing 'specific' to <=10 clusters still gives only 3/200). ~45% of the shared-token mass is outright ubiquitous (>=30 clusters); the rest is mid-frequency, never domain-rare.
conclusions: Confirms a-0033's generic-scaffolding-coincidence hypothesis with a hard number: the edit-distance conceptual-bridge detector matches on UNIVERSAL proof scaffolding (intro/simp/exact/rw...), not on domain-specific shared concepts. 199/200 of its MIN-closest outputs share ZERO domain-rare tactic tokens -- the same rate as random cross-cluster pairs -- which is precisely why it cannot recover Brouwer<->Lawvere-style conceptual bridges (a-0009): normalized-tactic edit distance is dominated by scaffolding common to all domains. This concretizes the central open problem (a-0001): a genuine conceptual-bridge detector must key on domain-specific shared STRUCTURE, downweighting ubiquitous scaffolding (e.g. IDF/TF-IDF weighting of tactic tokens, or aligning only rare tokens), not raw tactic-sequence similarity. Caveats: same top-30-cluster/top-30-longest-proof high-similarity sample as the detector; genericity measured at the single-token level (not n-gram), so longer domain-specific tactic MOTIFS built from individually-generic tokens are not captured -- a natural follow-up.
